title: erythroxylum subrotundum
intro: Erythroxylum subrotundum is a plant species belonging to the Erythroxylum genus, commonly known as the 'Peruvian coca' due to its similarity in appearance to the coca plant. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a local anesthetic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory. The leaves of the Erythroxylum subrotundum plant have been used to treat various health issues, including headaches, fever, and digestive problems, showcasing its potential health benefits. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness their medicinal properties.
